Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Peculiar microstructural evolution and tensile properties of $ \beta $-containing $ \gamma $-TiAl alloys fabricated by electron beam melting

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: ICP-OES for metallic elements; inert gas fusion for oxygen

Composition_Info.Note: Raw powder composition: Ti-44.8Al-4.0Cr-0.15O (at%). Al content decreases by ~0.9 at% during EBM fabrication due to evaporation.

Process_Text.original: Rectangular rods (10×10×30 mm) fabricated using Arcam Q10 EBM system at accelerating voltage 60 kV, preheating temperature 1333 K, layer thickness 90 μm, beam current 28 mA, scanning speed 800 mm/s, energy density 4.0 J/mm². Building direction parallel to longitudinal direction of rods. No post-annealing or HIP treatment.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM Arcam Q10, 60 kV, 1333 K preheat, 90 μm layer, ED=4.0 J/mm² (high energy density condition)

Microstructure_Text.original: Uniform α₂/β/γ mixed structure composed of two regions: (1) α₂/γ lamellar region and (2) β/γ dual phase region with ~50% β phase. Volume fraction of β phase in β/γ dual phase region is ~50%. Compared to low ED microstructure, this HED microstructure has coarser lamellar spacing (λ = 62.1 nm vs 32.5 nm).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Uniform HED microstructure with α₂/β/γ mixed structure; α₂/γ lamellar region and β/γ dual phase region (~50% each)

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: BF images and SAED patterns of β phase (B=[110]β), lamellar structure in HED microstructure. Dislocations and twins observed in γ phase at 1023 K.
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM_BSE: α2 phase appears gray, γ phase appears black, β phase appears white in BSE contrast

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K (RT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM-like specimen, gauge dimensions 5.0×1.5×0.5 mm, cut from center of rods, loading axis parallel to building direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile strength exceeds cast 48-2-2 alloys (450-550 MPa) and comparable to cast TNM alloys (700-800 MPa)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 1023 K (HT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM-like specimen, gauge dimensions 5.0×1.5×0.5 mm, cut from center of rods, loading axis parallel to building direction
Properties_Info.Note: Higher than cast 48-2-2 (~450 MPa) and TNM alloys (~600 MPa)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K (RT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Note: Less than 1% elongation at RT regardless of process parameters; limited plastic deformation due to brittle fracture, not defects

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 1023 K (HT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Note: Approximately 40% elongation at 1023 K, significantly higher than cast alloys (~10%)

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: ICP-OES for metallic elements; inert gas fusion for oxygen

Composition_Info.Note: Raw powder composition: Ti-44.8Al-4.0Cr-0.15O (at%). Al content decreases by ~0.9 at% during EBM fabrication due to evaporation.

Process_Text.original: Rectangular rods (10×10×30 mm) fabricated using Arcam Q10 EBM system at accelerating voltage 60 kV, preheating temperature 1333 K, layer thickness 90 μm, beam current 6 mA, scanning speed 5000 mm/s, energy density 1.4 J/mm². Building direction parallel to longitudinal direction of rods. No post-annealing or HIP treatment.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM Arcam Q10, 60 kV, 1333 K preheat, 90 μm layer, ED=1.4 J/mm² (low energy density condition)

Microstructure_Text.original: Layered microstructure perpendicular to building direction, composed of alternating layers A and B (each ~45 μm wide). Layer A: ultrafine α₂/γ lamellar grains with jagged grain boundaries + β/γ cell structure. Layer B: α₂/β/γ mixed structure (same as HED microstructure). Ultrafine lamellar spacing (λ = 32.5 nm) is smaller than HED microstructure (62.1 nm) due to lower Al content in massive α grains. β/γ cell structure has ~75% γ phase volume fraction. Formation mechanism: rapid cooling leads to massive α transformation (β→α) without β+α phase separation.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: LED layered microstructure with ultrafine α₂/γ lamellar grains (λ=32.5 nm) in layer A and α₂/β/γ mixed structure in layer B; each layer ~45 μm wide

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: BF images and SAED patterns of lamellar structure; massive α grain formation confirmed by jagged grain boundaries
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM_BSE: α2 phase appears gray, γ phase appears black, β phase appears white in BSE contrast
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.OM: Cross-section observations for defect and microstructure analysis

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K (RT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM-like specimen, gauge dimensions 5.0×1.5×0.5 mm, cut from center of rods, loading axis parallel to building direction
Properties_Info.Note: Strength depends strongly on volume fraction of ultrafine α₂/γ lamellar grains (Vα2/γG)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 1023 K (HT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM-like specimen, gauge dimensions 5.0×1.5×0.5 mm, cut from center of rods, loading axis parallel to building direction
Properties_Info.Note: HT strength maintained despite lower β phase content due to solid solution strengthening from high Cr in ultrafine lamellar grains

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K (RT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Note: Less than 1% elongation at RT regardless of process parameters; brittle fracture

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 1023 K (HT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Note: Approximately 40% elongation at 1023 K, significantly higher than cast alloys (~10%)

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: ICP-OES for metallic elements; inert gas fusion for oxygen

Composition_Info.Note: Raw powder composition: Ti-44.8Al-4.0Cr-0.15O (at%). Al content decreases by ~0.9 at% during EBM fabrication. Intermediate ED condition (3.2 J/mm²).

Process_Text.original: Rectangular rods (10×10×30 mm) fabricated using Arcam Q10 EBM system at accelerating voltage 60 kV, preheating temperature 1333 K, layer thickness 90 μm, beam current ~15 mA, scanning speed ~1500 mm/s, energy density 3.2 J/mm². Building direction parallel to longitudinal direction of rods. No post-annealing or HIP treatment.
Process_Text.simplified: EBM Arcam Q10, 60 kV, 1333 K preheat, 90 μm layer, ED=3.2 J/mm² (intermediate/transitional energy density)

Microstructure_Text.original: Microstructure transition stage between LED and HED types. Contains mixture of layered microstructure features and α₂/β/γ mixed structure. Approaching ED boundary of ~3 J/mm².
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Transitional microstructure between LED and HED types; mixed features of both microstructural types

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K (RT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM-like specimen, gauge dimensions 5.0×1.5×0.5 mm, cut from center of rods, loading axis parallel to building direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT strength intermediate between R1 (773 MPa) and R5 (higher strength at low ED)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 1023 K (HT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM-like specimen, gauge dimensions 5.0×1.5×0.5 mm, cut from center of rods, loading axis parallel to building direction
Properties_Info.Note: HT strength nearly constant up to ~3 J/mm² then increases with higher ED

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298 K (RT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Note: Less than 1% elongation at RT regardless of process parameters; brittle fracture

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 1023 K (HT); strain rate 1.7×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹; vacuum
Properties_Info.Note: Approximately 40% elongation at 1023 K regardless of fabrication conditions

Measured_Composition.Measurement_Method: ICP-OES for metallic elements; inert gas fusion for oxygen

Composition_Info.Note: Raw powder fabricated by Ar gas atomization from master ingots (Kobe Steel). Al content adjusted ~1 at% higher than nominal to account for evaporation during EBM. O content derived from surface oxide layer.

Process_Text.original: Raw powder fabricated by Ar gas atomization from master ingots of Ti-44Al-4Cr alloy provided by Kobe Steel, Ltd. Atomization performed at Osaka Yakin Kogyo Co., Ltd., Japan.
Process_Text.simplified: Ar gas atomization from master ingots

Process_Info.Equipment: Ar gas atomization system (Osaka Yakin Kogyo Co., Ltd.)

Microstructure_Text.original: Spherical powder particles observed by SEM. Particles have circularity K = 0.94 indicating near-spherical shape. Mean diameter (dm) = 70.2 μm with single-peak narrow distribution. High flowability indicated by angle of repose ~44.8°. Small amounts of fine particles (≤10 μm) and satellites contribute to flowability.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Spherical gas-atomized powder; mean diameter 70.2 μm; K=0.94; high flowability

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM: Morphology observation of raw powder particles
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.ImageJ: Circularity measurement from projected area and perimeter (K = 4πA/P²)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Measured using revolution powder analyzer; indicates powder flowability
Properties_Info.Note: Angle of repose comparable to other reported EBM powders; indicates good flowability

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Measured by laser diffraction particle size distribution analyzer; at least 200 particles measured
Properties_Info.Note: Single peak distribution with narrow width

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Calculated from K = 4πA/P² where A = projected area, P = perimeter; measured using ImageJ software; at least 200 particles
